project.objective: Identify the parents of Patrick Flynn, born ca. 1845 in Pennsylvania, died 1908 in Schuylkill County, PA

questions.question: Who were the parents of Patrick Flynn (b. ~1845, PA, d. 1908)?
questions.rationale: This is the primary research objective.

questions.question: Where was Patrick Flynn in the 1850 census?
questions.rationale: The 1850 census is the earliest enumeration where Patrick would appear by name (age ~5). Locating him in a household identifies candidate parents.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Searched 1850 census for Schuylkill County on FamilySearch (indexed and browse), Ancestry (indexed), and MyHeritage (indexed). The search located the Thomas Flynn household (dwelling 84) and also surfaced a second Patrick Flynn of similar age in household 197 — which of the two is the subject is tracked as identity conflict c_002.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — Patrick Flynn located in a specific 1850 household, answering the question.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: Three major repositories searched (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage). No additional known indexes of the 1850 Schuylkill County census exist.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: FamilySearch provides the original census image; Ancestry index is derivative but confirmed consistent.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: FamilySearch (original) and Ancestry (derivative) both return the same household. Two access paths, one underlying original.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Yes — FamilySearch provides original census image with indeterminate-quality information.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: No conflicts on locating candidate households; the two-candidate identity question is tracked as c_002.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Open on identity — a second Patrick Flynn of similar age (household 197) exists in the county, and no later record from either household has been located; which is the subject is unresolved (c_002).

items.rationale: 1850 census fully indexed on FamilySearch. Free access, start here.
Ancestry has independent indexing; cross-check for transcription errors.
Third independent index for coverage confirmation.
Confirm Patrick still in Thomas Flynn household in 1860. Strengthens parent-child identification.
Death certificate may name parents directly.
Thomas Flynn probate/will may name Patrick as son.

log.notes: Found Patrick Flynn age 5 in household of Thomas Flynn, dwelling 84, Schuylkill Co. Three other Flynn results examined — ages and locations don't match.
Ancestry index confirms same household. Transcription matches FamilySearch except Ancestry reads dwelling as 84, FamilySearch as 84 — consistent.
MyHeritage returned no results for Patrick Flynn in 1850 Schuylkill County. Site may not have this collection indexed. Searched broader Pennsylvania — still no match.

Found a Patrick Flynn enumerated in Allegheny County (Pittsburgh area) in 1870 — same name, roughly consistent age, but ~320 km west of the subject's known Schuylkill County residence. Flagged as a possible same-name individual pending geographic-feasibility analysis.

sources.citation: 1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, dwelling 84, family 91, Thomas Flynn household; NARA microfilm publication M432, roll 810; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 1 May 2026.
sources.notes: Image quality good. Enumerator handwriting clear.

sources.citation: 1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, dwelling 84, Thomas Flynn household; digital image, Ancestry.com, accessed 1 May 2026.

citation_detail.what: 1850 U.S. Federal Census, population schedule (Ancestry index)

sources.notes: Ancestry's index of the same original census. Transcription consistent with FamilySearch.

sources.citation: 1870 U.S. Census, Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, Patrick Flynn household; NARA microfilm publication M593; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 4 May 2026.
sources.notes: A Patrick Flynn in Allegheny County, 1870. Identity vs. the Schuylkill County subject is unresolved (see c_003).

assertions.informant: Unknown household member reporting to census enumerator
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Census enumerator is the recorder, not the informant. The household member who provided the name is unknown. Proximity is 'unknown' rather than 'household_member' because for names specifically, the enumerator may have read a sign, heard a neighbor, or made an assumption — the name fact doesn't necessarily require active reporting the way age/birthplace does.

assertions.informant: Unknown household member (likely Thomas Flynn or wife)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Age and birthplace require active reporting by a household member — someone had to tell the enumerator these facts. Proximity is 'household_member' rather than 'unknown' because these facts could only come from someone in the household.

assertions.informant: Census enumerator (witness to residence by visiting the dwelling)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: For residence facts specifically, the enumerator is a direct witness — they physically visited the dwelling and recorded who lived there. This distinguishes residence from other census facts where the household member is the true informant.

assertions.value: Listed in household of Thomas Flynn (head), position consistent with child
assertions.informant: Inferred from household structure — no explicit informant for relationships in 1850 census
assertions.informant_bias_notes: 1850 census does not state relationships; this assertion is inferred from household position and shared surname, not directly reported by any informant. The relationship is indirect evidence constructed by the researcher, not information provided by a census informant.

assertions.informant: Unknown household member (likely Thomas Flynn himself) reporting to census enumerator
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Census enumerator is the recorder, not the informant. As head of household, Thomas likely provided his own name, but the 1850 census does not identify the informant.

assertions.informant: Census enumerator (witness to residence by visiting the dwelling)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Enumerator is a direct witness to residence. This records a Patrick Flynn in Allegheny County; whether he is the Schuylkill County subject is the open identity question (c_003).

person_evidence.rationale: Name matches (Patrick Flynn), age consistent with ~1845 birth, located in Schuylkill County where subject is known to have lived. Only Patrick Flynn of this age in the county in 1850.
Same record as pe_001, same person. Household position and shared surname with head Thomas Flynn suggest parent-child relationship, but 1850 census does not state relationships explicitly.

Same assertion as pe_002 but linked to Thomas Flynn (I2). The relationship assertion ('position consistent with child') equally bears on the head of household. Thomas Flynn is the other party in the implied parent-child relationship.
Thomas Flynn, head of household where Patrick was found. Age (~32 in 1850, born ~1818) is consistent with being Patrick's father. Name matches candidate father.

A Patrick Flynn appears in Allegheny County in 1870 — name matches, age roughly consistent — but the residence is ~320 km west of the subject's known Schuylkill County home, with no record bridging a move. Linked speculatively pending geographic-feasibility analysis (c_003).

conflicts.description: Which of the two Patrick Flynns recorded in 1850 Schuylkill County is the subject? Household 84 (age 5, head Thomas Flynn) vs. household 197 (age 6, head not named in the index). No later record from either household has been located to disambiguate.
conflicts.identity_question: Is the Patrick Flynn enumerated in the Thomas Flynn 1850 household (dwelling 84) the same person as the subject Patrick Flynn (I1), rather than the same-age Patrick Flynn in household 197?

conflicts.description: An 1870 U.S. Census record (src_005, a_014) places a Patrick Flynn in Allegheny County, ~320 km west of the subject's documented Schuylkill County residences (1850, 1860). Is this the same Patrick Flynn (who would have moved west) or a same-named individual?
conflicts.identity_question: Is the Patrick Flynn in the 1870 Allegheny County census (a_014) the same person as the subject Patrick Flynn (I1) documented in Schuylkill County?

hypotheses.claim: Patrick Flynn's father was Thomas Flynn of Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
hypotheses.notes: Single piece of evidence so far: 1850 census co-enumeration (indirect — relationship inferred from household position). The 1860 census, death certificate, and probate searches have not yet been performed, and identity conflict c_002 (two same-age Patrick Flynns in the county) is unresolved.

timelines.label: Patrick Flynn — assuming Thomas Flynn parentage

events.description: Born in Ireland, estimated from census ages
Enumerated age 5 in Thomas Flynn household, dwelling 84
